# Clinician Review

> Use this template for changes to evidence claims, dosing, contraindications,
> exclusions, citations, or guideline anchors. For pure code changes (build,
> tooling, refactors) use the standard Code Change template.

## What is changing

<!-- One sentence: the specific claim, exclusion, or recommendation being
     added, removed, or modified. -->

## Why

<!-- The clinical rationale. What evidence supports this change?
     What's the harm scenario this prevents (or the false-negative this fixes)? -->

## Citation

<!-- Required for any change to evidence, dosing, exclusions, or recommendations.
     DOI or PMID preferred. Include study title and year. -->

- Source:
- DOI / PMID:
- Year:
- Quality (RCT / systematic review / observational / position statement):

## Last-reviewed date

<!-- When did you last verify the source? -->

## Conflict of interest disclosure

<!-- Required. Are you, your employer, your practice, or any organization
     you represent in a financial relationship with:
     - A supplement brand or distributor
     - A lab testing company
     - An advocacy organization with a position on this topic
     - A publication or conference being cited

     "None" is a valid answer. Hidden conflicts that surface later are
     grounds for reverting changes. -->

## Tests

<!-- Did you add or update a persona snapshot that exercises this change?
     Did you check that the existing invariant tests still pass?
     If neither, explain why. -->

- [ ] Persona snapshot added or updated
- [ ] Existing invariant tests still pass (`npm test`)
- [ ] Type check passes (`npm run typecheck`)

## Reviewer credit

<!-- How would you like to be credited in CONTRIBUTORS.md? -->

- Name:
- Credential (e.g. MD, PharmD, RDN):
- Optional practice / website link for the Trusted Reviewers page:
- I'd prefer to remain anonymous: [ ]
